name: Mayor's Health Line
blurb: Free phone service from the Boston Public Health Commission that connects residents to low-cost and free testing locations across the city. Call 617-534-5050 or toll-free 1-800-847-0710, Monday through Friday.

name: Boston Medical Center Patient Financial Services
blurb: BMC is Boston's largest safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured patients apply for MassHealth, Health Safety Net, and BMC's own payment-assistance program before or after lab testing.

name: LAC+USC Medical Center Financial Assistance
blurb: LAC+USC, run by LA County Department of Health Services, offers Ability-to-Pay financial assistance for uninsured residents. Counselors help with applications at the hospital and county clinics.

name: Cook County Health CareLink
blurb: Cook County Health's CareLink program offers discounted care, including labs, for uninsured Cook County residents based on income. Apply through any Cook County Health facility.

name: Valleywise Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Valleywise Health is Maricopa County's public safety-net system. Financial counselors help uninsured patients apply for AHCCCS, Valleywise's payment-assistance program, and sliding fee scales.

name: NYC Health + Hospitals Options Program
blurb: NYC Health + Hospitals operates the city's public hospital system. The Options program offers a sliding fee scale for uninsured New Yorkers, including labs, based on income and household size.

name: Parkland Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Parkland is Dallas County's public safety-net hospital. The Parkland Financial Assistance Program helps uninsured Dallas County residents qualify for discounted care, including labs.

name: Orlando Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Orlando Health offers a financial-assistance program for uninsured and underinsured patients. Counselors help with applications for charity care and Medicaid eligibility.

name: Tampa General Hospital Financial Assistance
blurb: Tampa General is the area's primary safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured patients apply for charity care, Medicaid, and the hospital's payment-assistance program.

name: Denver Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Denver Health is the city's main safety-net hospital. The Denver Health Financial Assistance Program offers discounted care, including labs, for uninsured residents based on income.

name: Grady Health System Financial Counseling
blurb: Grady is Atlanta's largest safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured Fulton and DeKalb residents apply for the Grady Card, Medicaid, and charity care.

name: Eskenazi Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Eskenazi Health is the Marion County safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured patients apply for the Eskenazi Health Advantage program, Medicaid, and HIP.

name: Atrium Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Atrium Health operates Charlotte's main safety-net facilities. Financial counselors help uninsured patients apply for charity care, Medicaid, and the hospital's payment-assistance program.

name: Nashville General Hospital Financial Assistance
blurb: Nashville General is the city's safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured Davidson County residents apply for charity care, TennCare, and sliding fee programs.

name: Henry Ford Health Financial Assistance
blurb: Henry Ford Health offers a financial-assistance program for uninsured and underinsured patients across Detroit. Counselors help with applications for charity care and Medicaid.

name: OhioHealth Financial Assistance
blurb: OhioHealth offers a financial-assistance program for uninsured and underinsured patients in central Ohio. Counselors help with applications for charity care and Medicaid.

name: UMC Southern Nevada Financial Assistance
blurb: University Medical Center is Clark County's public safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured residents apply for Medicaid, charity care, and payment plans.

name: Harris Health Financial Assistance Program
blurb: Harris Health is the public safety-net system for Harris County. The Financial Assistance Program covers labs and primary care for uninsured residents based on income.

name: Jackson Health System Financial Assistance
blurb: Jackson Health is Miami-Dade's public safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured residents apply for the Jackson Charity Care program and Medicaid.

name: University Health Financial Assistance
blurb: University Health is Bexar County's public safety-net system. CareLink offers discounted care, including labs, for uninsured Bexar County residents based on income.

name: Zuckerberg San Francisco General Financial Counseling
blurb: ZSFG is San Francisco's public safety-net hospital. Financial counselors help uninsured residents apply for Healthy San Francisco, Medi-Cal, and the hospital's payment-assistance program.

name: UCHealth Financial Assistance
blurb: UCHealth, including Memorial Hospital in Colorado Springs, offers a financial-assistance program for uninsured and underinsured patients based on income.

name: JPS Health Network Financial Assistance
blurb: JPS Health Network is Tarrant County's public safety-net hospital system. JPS Connection offers discounted care, including labs, for uninsured county residents based on income.

name: Philadelphia Department of Public Health Health Centers
blurb: Philadelphia operates eight District Health Centers offering low-cost primary care, labs, and STD and HIV testing on a sliding fee scale. Locations are listed on the city site.

name: Alameda Health System Financial Counseling
blurb: Alameda Health System, including Highland Hospital, is the county safety-net system. Financial counselors help uninsured residents apply for Medi-Cal, HealthPAC, and charity care.
